items.title: 'Aston Villa heeft vervanger Malen bijna binnen door akkoord met Besiktas'
items.summary: Tammy Abraham staat op het punt te vertrekken bij Besiktas. Aston Villa nadert volgens Sky Sports een akkoord met de Turkse topclub over een transfer van de 28-jarige spits, die in de selectie de plek van de vertrokken Donyell Malen moet opvullen.

items.title: Feyenoord beloont Valente voor ontwikkeling met nieuw contract
items.summary: Luciano Valente heeft een half jaar na zijn komst al een nieuw contract getekend bij Feyenoord. De 22-jarige middenvelder ligt nu tot medio 2030 vast in Rotterdam.

items.title: Feyenoord voor dilemmaweek: 'Geen ruimte voor roulatie tegen Betis én PSV'
items.summary: Feyenoord won donderdagavond met overtuigende cijfers van Sturm Graz: 3-0. Voor het eerst sinds 19 oktober werd de nul gehouden en de weg omhoog lijkt ingezet. De Rotterdammers hebben na het duel met Heracles Almelo van zondag een zwaar programma voor de boeg. Met name de week van 26 januari, waarin het Real Betis én PSV treft, wordt cruciaal. Hoe zit het met de belastbaarheid van spelers? En kan Robin van Persie prioriteiten stellen voor een wedstrijd?

items.title: Spurs in talks for Robertson, Villa close on Abraham return: football news – live
items.summary: ⚽ The latest football news heading into the weekend ⚽ Premier League: 10 things to look out for | Mail John This weekend’s Premier League games are the reverse fixtures of the season openers, back in sun-soaked August. Looking back, the most jarring result was Nottingham Forest 3-1 Brentford, with the teams taking very different trajectories since then. “It was certainly a tough day for us all, but it is pretty obvious to see the development that we have made in the months since that, and I am delighted with the progress we have made,” Brentford manager Keith Andrews said yesterday. Continue reading...

items.title: Saibari schuldbewust na Afrika Cup-rel: 'Het zag er slecht uit'
items.summary: Ismael Saibari beseft dat hij fout zat tijdens het handdoekincident in de Afrika Cup-finale tegen Senegal. De Marokkaanse middenvelder van PSV wil het voorval nu achter zich laten.

items.title: Ten Cate belde met Menzo: 'Hij was blij dat Suriname bij mij was uitgekomen'
items.summary: Bondscoach Henk ten Cate van Suriname heeft gebeld met zijn voorganger Stanley Menzo toen hij benaderd werd door de bond om het land richting een eventueel WK te leiden.

items.title: Premier League Power Rankings: Rating all 20 teams on performances, not results
items.summary: In the debut of ESPN's Premier League Power Rankings, a look at all 20 teams, including why Liverpool, Chelsea and Tottenham are better than the table suggests.

items.summary: Arsenal are exploring the possibility of launching a summer move for Atletico Madrid forward Julián Álvarez, sources have told ESPN.

items.title: Rodman contract saga ends with record Spirit deal
items.summary: United States forward Trinity Rodman signed a record three-year contract with the Washington Spirit on Thursday to remain at the club, ending months-long uncertainty over the star's future.

items.title: Williams to miss first 2026 F1 test with car not ready
items.summary: Williams will miss the first pre-season testing event of the 2026 Formula 1 season after failing to have their car ready for the Barcelona shakedown.

items.title: Spillane's Tower fancied to see off Grey Dawning on Cheltenham Trials Day
items.summary: The Weekend Winners team are back, looking ahead to Cheltenham Trials Day as well as the Great Yorkshire Chase from Doncaster.

items.title: Fans’ anger after Braga flop shows Forest honeymoon is over for Dyche
items.summary: Initial optimism has faded after arrival of former Burnley manager, who needs to get more from a struggling squad There was plenty for Sean Dyche to stew on as he marched off the pitch at the Estádio Municipal de Braga with the frustrations of the Nottingham Forest supporters ringing in his ears. The boos were not universal, but fans who follow the team over land and sea are usually a decent barometer of the prevailing feeling. By the end of a sodden, joyless night in northern Portugal, it felt like false advertising, possibly one for trading standards. It wasn’t supposed to be like this? Dyche cannot be blamed for the dismal conditions but he and the players can be for a listless performance. Beyond a likely Europa League playoff, which will mean another away day to squeeze into the schedule, fans are seemingly concerned where this is all going. Forest were devoid of sharp edges and not only in attack, where the absence of a bona fide striker was painfully apparent. It was a miserable night encapsulated by second-half events: Morgan Gibbs-White’s penalty miss, a Ryan Yates own goal 54 seconds later, Dan Ndoye being booked for simulation and Elliot Anderson being sent off. Continue reading...

items.title: Spurs open talks to sign Liverpool's Robertson
items.summary: Tottenham enter into talks with Liverpool as they look to secure a deal for left-back Andy Robertson.

items.title: Chelsea’s Alyssa Thompson: ‘I think of this as like going away for college like all my friends did’
items.summary: Blues’ record signing on adapting to English football culture, the challenge of leaving family behind and being well taught by Sonia Bompastor Alyssa Thompson is no stranger to the limelight. Despite being only 21, her undeniable natural talent and eye‑catching career have propelled her into the headlines ever since her hometown club, Angel City, made her the first pick in the 2023 National Women’s Soccer League (NWSL) draft. The past few months, however, have provided the USA international with a different challenge altogether. A high-profile deadline day move to the English champions, Chelsea, in September meant leaving her family and the comforts of Los Angeles and testing herself overseas. Continue reading...

items.title: Uncapped trio named in England Six Nations squad
items.summary: Steve Borthwick has named three uncapped players in Greg Fisilau, Vilikesa Sela and Emmanuel Iyogun as part of his 36-player England Six Nations squad.

items.title: Celtic pick up where they left off under O'Neill - Trusty

items.title: Rodman 'becomes highest-paid women's footballer'
items.summary: Trinity Rodman signs a new three-year deal with Washington Spirit that will reportedly make her the world's highest-paid female footballer.
